titleStmt.title: Paris, Bibliothèque nationale de France, BnF Éthiopien d'Abbadie 114
Taʾammǝra Māryām

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

licence.p: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

msItem.title: Taʾammǝra Māryām
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi collection, Title
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi collection, Miracle of Hildephonsus (Dexius) of Toledo
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Hildephonsus (Dexius) of Toledo
Soba ʾaṣḥafa Daqsǝyos taʾammǝrǝki qǝddusa
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1108MMDaqseyos)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi collection, Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a poor monk whom Mary gave a garment
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a poor monk whom Mary gave a garment
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 1: Introduction
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1145MMGarment)
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi collection, Miracle of a picture of Mary speaking to a worker
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a picture of Mary speaking to a worker
ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi, Stanza 2, referring to
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1109MMWorker)

Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a Jew of ʾAḫmīm

Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi collection, Miniature (same as AT1110MMJew)

Malkǝʾ-hymn to the Cross, John the Evangelist, and the Apostles
Malkǝʾa śǝʿl
Malkǝʾ-hymn to Our Lady Mary

supportDesc.foliation: There is foliation provided in the upper right corner of each folio.

collation.note: The quire structure with this manuscript is not discernible due to the tightness of the binding and the quality of the images.

layout.ab: Primary pricks are visible, horizontal pricks are visible.
The upper line is written above the ruling, the lower line is written above the ruling.

handNote.seg: The name St Mary (and other holy names), the first two lines of the explicits of the narrations and stanzas, and all the legends of the miniatures are rubricated.

decoDesc.summary: The manuscript is richly decorated with multiple miniatures mainly belong to the so called First Gondarine style of decoration. Paintings in the manuscript appear after their corresponding narrations as means of illustration for the texts. Another artist with different style of decoration ( second Gondarine style of decoration) added fifteen full page images illustrating scenes from the New Testament as well as illustrations. portraying saints, martyrs and Ethiopian Solomonic kings.

decoNote.desc: A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Daqsǝyos with two episodes, showing: first Hildephonsus of Toledo who is sitting on a throne and giving the book of the Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Hildephonsus (Dexius) of Toledo to St Mary who is sitting near to him on a throne, and second, Hildephonsus of Toledo receiving a gift of vestment and throne from St Mary.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Daqsǝyos with two episodes, showing: first, Hildephonsus of Toledo's successor who is sitting on a throne reads the Miracle of Mary: Miracle of Hildephonsus (Dexius) of Toledo and second, the successor bishop falling and being smitten by Raguel for having touched St Mary gifts to Hildephonsus of Toledo without permission.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Miracle of the poor monk to whom Mary gave a garment with two episodes, showing: first, the poor monk praying to Virgin and Child; second, St Mary giving the poor monk garment and food.

A miniature of the art themeMiracle of Mary: Mary speaks to a worker with two episodes, showing: first, the peasant standing and praying near to the icon Virgin and Child and second, St Mary who is carrying her Child, in the same depiction blessing the peasant.

A miniature illustrating Miracle of Mary: The Jew of ʾAkmim with four episodes, showing: first, the Jewish priest with crossed hands praying in front of St Mary who is sitting on a throne with the two deacons in the background; second, the Jewish priest lying on the ground after his fall from excess bowing and third, St Mary raising the Jewish priest by her hand.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Dǝmyanos who wrote Mary's name in golden ink with of two episodes, showing: first, Dǝmyānos writing the name of St Mary in a manuscript and second, Dǝmyānos lying down before the icon of Mary, performing a gesture of blessing (in the background, the pillar of gold, on which St Mary inscribed Dǝmyānos' name is depicted).

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: ʾAbbās, bishop of Rome with three episodes, showing: first, the bishop ʾAbbās standing and distributing holy communion surrounded by the faithful, among whom is the woman who kisses his hand; second, the bishop sitting and cutting off his hand, and third, the bishop and the faithful standing in front of the icon of the Virgin and Child, which has healed his hand.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The monk, Yǝsḥaq, who prayed for seven years that St Mary would appear to him with two episodes showing: first, the monk Yǝsḥaq falling on the ground during the apparition of Mary, and second, the icon of St Mary taking Yǝsḥaq by the hand and raising him.
A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Māryām of Dafrā with one episode, showing: St Mary standing next to Māryām of Dafrā where the house represented with Jerusalem is depicted near to her.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The French artist who fell from the scaffolding while painting frescoes with three episodes, showing: first, the painter sitting on a scaffold and drawing a scene; second, the painter falling down from the scaffold and third, the icon of St Mary extending her hand to save the painter.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Mary carries a sick man to Jerusalem with four episodes, showing, first, the sick pilgrim lying in bed; second the sick pilgrim standing before the places to which St Mary carries him; third, Golgotha, Jerusalem, the mount of Olives, Bethlehem and the Jordan are depicted; and fourth, St Mary is standing in the middle of the places depicted with a house scene background.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Zakāryās, who used to bring roses to the icon of St Mary with five episodes, showing from the bottom left to the right: first, Zakārǝyās praying the greeting of the angel before the icon of Virgin and Child; second, Zakārǝyās nailing down in front of the icon of Virgin and Child; third, Zakārǝyās decorating Saint Mary's crown in the icon of Virgin and Child with roses; fourth, St Mary who is sitting on a throne surrounded by angels and the five virgins (carrying roses) and collects roses out of his mouth and fifth, the thieves being consecrated as monks.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Yolyānā and Barbārā from Bethlehem with two episodes, showing: first, the two thieves attempting to eat the bread they stole from Yolyānā and Barbārā and second, depicts the two thieves standing before an icon of St Mary and are being consecrated as monks.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The three Arabs journeying to Rif and Salomon, the abbot of Qalamon with six episodes, showing: first, the three Arabs swimming in the water; second, one man being eaten by a crocodile and fish surrounding the river; third, the two others standing on a rock above the water; fourth, one man riding a camel to the monastery, which is depicted in the background; fifth, the other man bringing his sheep's wool and sixth, the camel brought by the Arab eating from a tree.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The church of ʿƎqonā, which was removed to the edge of the sea with three episodes, showing: first, St Mary surrounded by the sleeping monks of the monastery, which is depicted in the background; second, the monastery with its monks that has been moved to the shore of the see and third, a group of people marvelling at the miracle.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The blind priest, Yoḥannǝs Bakansi with two episodes, showing: first, the priest Yoḥannǝs is depicted standing and praying before the icon of the Virgin and Childand second, St Mary taking out her breast and dropping her milk into the eyes of Yoḥannǝs.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The rich man from Qʷalāsis who fought the Qʷǝz and whose eye was pierced by an arrow with four episodes, showing: first, the rich man from Qʷalāsis travelling with his companions on a ship; second, four individuals from Qʷǝz piercing the eye of the noble man; third, the rich man fallen after his eye has been pierced by arrows from the enemies and fourth, the rich man standing next to a small statue of himself in front of the icon of the Virgin and Child.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Elizabeth, the blind daughter of Abraham and Gerā ʾAnǝśt of Badǝrmān with four episodes, showing: first, the woman and her blind daughter standing and praying before the icon of the Virgin and Child ; second, both lying asleep before the icon of Virgin and Child; third St Mary waking up the girl, and fourth, St Mary giving sight to the blind girl by dropping her breast's milk.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The widow who could not find husbands for her three daughters with three episodes, showing: first, the poor widow praying before the the icon of the Virgin and Child where her three daughters are sitting below her; second, the funeral of the eldest daughter, who is wrapped in a shroud and surrounded by persons, and third, the two other daughters standing next to the arborist man called Tewos.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The two brothers who copied the Miracles of Mary with four episodes, showing: first, the two brothers sitting and writing next to each other; second, the demons hanging the soul of one of the scribes; third, St Mary pulling down the scribe's soul from the demons; fourth, the scribe sitting again and resuming his writing with his brother.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Giyorgis Ḥaddis, who was delivered from prison with two episodes, showing: first, Giyorgis lying in prison bound at his hands and feet, with a dove appearing to him; second, Giyorgis standing healed from his wounds.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Kaṭir, the old priest at ʾƎlkǝsus with three episodes, showing: first, the custodian of the church standing in front of the sitting Kaṭir ordering him to leave the church; second, St Mary sitting on a throne surrounded by two with the custodian of the church sitting before her; third, the healed Kaṭir standing before St Mary, who is sitting on throne.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The two brothers Tāg and Nazib from Dalgā who were dyers with seven episodes, showing: first, the two brothers and the Muslim customer gathered around a piece of cloth; second, the Muslim holding the cloth of Nazib with an interest to kiss him for preparing him cloths; third, the Muslim denouncing Nazib to the judge; fourth, Nazib lying in prison bound at his hands and feet; fifth, St George of Lydda riding on a horse, brandishing a weapon, and appearing to the judge; sixth, the judge handing to Nazib his paper of release; and seventh, Nazib entering the church, in which the people are gathered, and handing his paper of release to the priest.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The stone-footed man from France with three episodes/events, showing: first, the bishop sitting on a throne before the people, who are gathered in a church, and amongst whom is the man with the petrified foot; second, the man with the petrified foot standing and praying before an icon of the Virgin and Child while his foot is healed; third, the man showing his healed foot to the bishop with the people standing in the background.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Bishop Mercurius, whom the patriarch wanted to remove because of leprosy with seven episodes, showing: first, the leprous bishop Marqorewos standing before Patriarch Zakārǝyās and showing him his leprosy; second, the patriarch warning Merqorewos not to deliver liturgical service; third, Marqorewos praying before the icon of the Virgin and Child; fourth, Virgin and Child healing the leprous bishop and performing gesture of blessing; fifth, the bishop showing himself healed to patriarch Zakārǝyās; sixth, the bishop showing himself healed to his student ; and seventh, the healed bishop distributing the Eucharist.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The woman with a broken foot from Hartarom with four episodes, showing: first, two men carrying the woman with a broken foot on a stretcher; second, the woman lying below the icon of the Virgin and Child; third, the broken foot woman again layed below the icon of Virgin and Child on another episode; fourth, the custodian looking the broken woman who is standing near to him with the icon of Virgin and Child depicted from the other side.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Sophia, abbess of Mount Carmel, who became pregnantwith seven episodes, showing: first, Sofǝyā lying on top of a building before the icon of the Virgin and Child and St Mary ordering an to take the child from Sofǝyā's womb ; second, an carries away the child from Sofǝyā's womb; third, the giving the child to Finkǝs to adopt him; fourth, the bishop sitting on a throne in front of a group of women accusing Sofǝyā; fifth, the bishop and Sofǝyā sitting next to each other discussing about the manner of the pregnancy; sixth, the bishop receiving the child from the hands of Finkǝs, sitting near to the bishop; and seventh, Sofǝyā's Child, who later become sucessessor of the former bishop (died some time before), surrounded by three persons.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Bārok of Finǝqi whom his enemies could not kill until he had confessed himself to a priest with seven episodes, showing: first, Bārok giving a wine in a vessel to his servant to distribute it to the guests; second, his guests sitting and drinking wine; third, the gusts eating food in Bārok's house; fourth; Bārok's enemies attacking him holding several weapons; fifth, a depiction of the icon of the Virgin and Child; sixth, Bārok being consecrated as a monk; seventh, the funeral of Bārok.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The deacon ʾAnǝsṭāsyos who recited the prayer tafaśśǝḥi and was healed by Mary with two episodes, showing: first, ʾAnǝsṭāsyos standing and praying in a church where the icon of the Virgin and Child depicted in front of him; second,ʾAnǝsṭāsyos lying before the icon of Virgin and Child, who is extending her hand towards him and announcing him good news concerning his illness.

A miniature of the art theme No item: AAT1135MMSalusi with four episodes, showing: first, the monk Sālusi surrounded by other monks, who are attacking him; second, the monks preventing him to eat; third, the monk Sālusi standing and praying in a church before the icon of the Virgin and Child and fourth, the monk Sālusi stood up near to the Church after penetrating the wall of the Church with his skull-cap fallen to the ground.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The cannibal of Qǝmǝr with eight episodes, showing: first, the cannibal surrounded by the bodies of those he killed; second the cannibal asking the peasant to sell his ox; third, the peasant and other inhabitants fleeing from the cannibal, with several houses depicted in the background; fourth, the cannibal offering water to a beggar covered by sores of leprosy; fifth, Jesus Christ, sitting on a throne ordering the demons to add the soul of the cannibal to the hell and the demons holding the soul of the cannibal; sixth; two weighing the cannibal's good and bad deeds in a scale; seventh, St Mary requesting her beloved Son Jesus Christ to give mercy for the soul of the cannibal, and eighth, St Mary holding the soul of the cannibal.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The son of a widow, who became a thief, is saved by Mary with four episodes, showing: first, the widow's son stealing cloth from a church; second, members of the Church arresting and starting to punish the thief; third, St Mary ordering the punishers to let him free, and fourth, the thief standing in front of the icon of the St Mary who is depicted on a throne.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: The pregnant woman who was caught by the tide while going to the church of St Michael with three episodes, showing: first, the woman holding her new-born child protected by St Mary from the wave; second a church amongst the waves from which the people are fleeing, and third, the woman with her child surrounded by the marvelling people.

A miniature of the art theme Miracle of Mary: Mary gives a thirsty dog water to drink from her shoe with three episodes, showing: first, the thirsty dog searching for water and being kicked by one of the two women sitting near to the well surrounded by plants; second, St Mary giving the dog water to drink from her shoe and, third, the group of virgins who chase out the dog gathered around the well.

decoDesc.decoNote: locus target="#87v"/> A miniture depicting Queen Walatta Qirqos (the spouse of king Ḥǝzqǝyās of Bagemdǝr) praying and standing near to a book which is placed over a decorated throne.

decoNote.desc: These decorations are stylistically different from the illustrative miniatures and probably could be additions by another artist at some time after the completion of the first hand textual and decorative composition of the manuscript. They contain images of several saints, martyrs and other divine scenes.

item.desc: Cotton that are tied directly to the tops of the pages are used to protect each miniatures.
the name "Antoine d'Abbadie" is written possibly by the cataloguer with a pencil like script.
A circle stamp, with the name Antoine d’Abbadie [d’Arrast] in it is stamped in the first and last guard folios.

binding.decoNote: Blind-tooled leather cover, with textile inlays

history.provenance: This manuscript is a part of the collection gathered by Antoine d’Abbadie [d’Arrast] during his stay in Ethiopia and neighbouring regions in 1837-48.

bibl.title: Notice sur les manuscrits éthiopiens de la Collection d’Abbadie
Extrait du Journal Asiatique
Catalogue des manuscrits éthiopiens de la Collection Antoine d’Abbadie
Catalogue raisonné de manuscrits éthiopiens appartenant à Antoine d’Abbadie
An Analysis of 17th-Century Ethiopian Pigments

The Indigenous and the Foreign in Christian Ethiopian Art. On Portuguese-Ethiopian Contacts in the 16th-17th Centuries. Papers from the Fifth International Conference on the History of Ethiopian Art (Arrábida, 26–30 November 1999)
Composite and Multiple-Text Manuscripts: The Ethiopian Evidence
One-Volume Libraries: Composite and Multiple Text Manuscripts
Studies in Manuscript Cultures

projectDesc.p: Encoded according to TEI P5 Guidelines.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

t:revisionDesc.change: Added elements on the first miracle
Added elements on the second miracle
Added elements on the third miracle
Added elements on the three miracles
Edited folio numbers and typo errors
Added elements on a hymn and images
updated IDs on the narration abou the poor monk
Added elements on addtions and varia
